wine_bars.name: Weinhimmel Wein & Genuss
wine_bars.address: Untere Landstraße 35, 3500 Krems an der Donau, Austria
wine_bars.wine_program: Over 200 Austrian and international labels with a strong focus on Wachau, Kremstal and Kamptal Grüner Veltliner and Riesling. Curated themed tastings led by winemaker Wolfgang Gansdorfer.
wine_bars.description: A wine boutique and tasting bar in central Krems pairing over 200 Austrian and select international labels with regional charcuterie, truffles and cheese. Knowledgeable staff guide visitors through Smaragd vs Federspiel comparisons.

verified.address_quoted: Untere Landstraße 35, 3500 Krems an der Donau

wine_bars.name: Leopold Weinbar & Vinothek
wine_bars.address: Utzstraße 1, 3500 Krems an der Donau, Austria
wine_bars.wine_program: 100+ Austrian wines poured at vinothek prices with daily Wachau, Kamptal and Kremstal focus. Hot kitchen Monday to Saturday from 16:00 with Austrian wine-pairing plates.
wine_bars.description: The evening wine bar in Krems that stays open until 01:00, pairing over 100 Austrian labels with a warm kitchen. Wines are sold at shop prices, making this one of the best-value wine-by-the-glass programs in Lower Austria.

wine_bars.name: Alter Klosterkeller
wine_bars.wine_program: Wachau-focused wine list heavy on regional Grüner Veltliner and Riesling producers. Garden terrace with Danube views for warm-weather sipping.
wine_bars.description: A Heuriger wine tavern nestled against the medieval city walls of Dürnstein, open from the afternoon until midnight. The garden garden overlooks the vineyard terraces and the Danube, with a traditional cold-platter menu alongside regional wines.

wine_bars.name: Heuriger Müllner
wine_bars.address: Stadtgraben 50, 3500 Krems an der Donau, Austria
wine_bars.wine_program: Own-estate Kremstal wines plus selected Wachau producers. Traditional Heuriger program with cold buffet and seasonal hot specials.
wine_bars.description: One of the top wine taverns in the Krems valley, run by brothers Johann and Michael Müllner. The 500-year-old walls of this urban Heuriger in Krems's Stadtgraben set the backdrop for own-estate wines poured alongside cold platters.

wine_bars.name: Steigenberger Smaragd Bar & Vinotheque
wine_bars.address: Am Goldberg 2, 3500 Krems an der Donau, Austria
wine_bars.wine_program: Curated Wachau and Lower Austria wine bar with sky roof terrace. Structured Smaragd vs Federspiel comparison flights and a short list of international benchmarks.
wine_bars.description: The sky bar and wine lounge at Steigenberger Hotel & Spa Krems, perched on a vineyard rise above the old town with UNESCO landscape views. The Smaragd bar runs a focused all-Austrian wine list with evening flights.

verified.address_quoted: Am Goldberg 2, 3500, Krems an der Donau

wine_bars.name: Wachauerstube Loiben
wine_bars.wine_program: Wine list rich in Wachau Grüner Veltliner and Riesling, with a particular focus on wines from Weingut Tegernseerhof. Paired with refined tavern cuisine.
wine_bars.description: A carefully renovated country inn in Unterloiben with a Wachau-focused wine list and upscale tavern cuisine. The wine selection centres on Tegernseerhof and Dürnstein producers; Friday to Tuesday kitchen service.

wine_bars.name: Schloss Dürnstein Bar & Wine Cellar
wine_bars.wine_program: 400-year-old historic cellar showcasing hundreds of Wachau bottles. Bar list spotlights Vinea Wachau Smaragd producers alongside a curated international selection.
wine_bars.description: The historic wine cellar bar of the only 5-star hotel in Lower Austria, housed in a 17th-century castle overlooking the Danube. Over 400 years of accumulated Wachau bottles available in the cellar bar, which is accessible to day visitors.

wine_bars.name: Restaurant Löwenherz Wine Bar - Hotel Richard Löwenherz
wine_bars.wine_program: Focused Wachau and Austrian wine list served alongside Danube-view fine dining. Speciality in Riesling and Grüner Veltliner from Dürnstein and Loiben appellations.
wine_bars.description: The bar and restaurant of the 4-star Hotel Richard Löwenherz, occupying a former convent in the heart of Dürnstein. Third-generation Thiery family hospitality pairs fine Wachau wines with regional cuisine in a historic riverside setting.

wine_bars.name: Weingut Pfeffel Wine Parlours
wine_bars.wine_program: Two cosy wine parlours in the hotel serving exclusively own-estate Wachau wines in all three classifications: Steinfeder, Federspiel, and Smaragd. Blind tastings of house spirits at the bar from 21:00 with 3-night stays.
wine_bars.description: The intimate vine-room wine bars at Gartenhotel & Weingut Pfeffel, a family wine estate hotel 900 metres west of Dürnstein. Own-estate Wachau wines pour alongside garden-to-table cuisine; the bar offers rare blind tastings of house apricot and pear spirits.

wine_bars.name: Heuriger Denk / Thal-Wachau
wine_bars.address: Obere Bachgasse 74, 3610 Weißenkirchen in der Wachau, Austria
wine_bars.wine_program: Own-estate Weißenkirchen wines from Heuriger Denk plus the daily rotating Thal Wachau selection, covering the full Vinea Wachau classification spectrum.
wine_bars.description: A classic Wachau Heuriger opening on weekdays from 15:00 and weekends from 11:00. The garden overlooking the apricot orchards and terraced vineyards of Weißenkirchen makes this the quintessential Wachau wine-tavern experience.

wine_bars.name: Weinstube Nikolaihof Wachau
wine_bars.address: Nikolaigasse 3, 3512 Mautern an der Donau, Austria
wine_bars.wine_program: Exclusively Nikolaihof biodynamic Demeter-certified wines paired with Christine Saahs' traditional Austrian organic cuisine. Back-vintage rarities available by the bottle.
wine_bars.description: The seasonal wine tavern at Austria's oldest winery and the first biodynamic estate in the Wachau. Open late April to early November, Wednesday to Saturday, under a century-old tree in the garden; Christine Saahs' organic Austrian menu is inseparable from the wine.

wine_bars.name: Domäne Wachau Kellerschlössl Evening Heuriger
wine_bars.wine_program: Full Domäne Wachau wine range served in the baroque garden. Specialist evenings focus on vertical Smaragd comparisons during the two dedicated summer weekends.
signature_pours: Domäne Wachau Ried Kellerberg Riesling Smaragd
wine_bars.description: The sought-after pop-up Heuriger in the baroque Kellerschlössel garden, open only two weekends each summer with views of the Ried Kellerberg terraces. A Wachau-bucket-list evening combining wine flights with traditional regional snacks.

wine_bars.name: Bailoni Apricot Lounge - Steinertor
wine_bars.address: Schwedengasse 2, 3500 Krems an der Donau, Austria
wine_bars.wine_program: Austria's apricot liqueur tasting bar. The lounge serves Bailoni apricot aperitifs, Marillenfrizzante, apricot cake and dumplings alongside the full spirits portfolio in the historic Steinertor shop.
wine_bars.description: The Steinertor shop and Apricot Lounge of Bailoni, Austria's first Wachau apricot distillery founded 1872, in the heart of Krems. Fresh apricot dumplings, schnapps tastings and liqueur flights pair the history of Wachau distilling with seasonal apricot delicacies.

wine_bars.name: Vinothek Wein und Wachau - Gottwald
wine_bars.wine_program: Curated Wachau and Kremstal selection from small-production growers, celebrating 25 years of independent wine retail. Quality-and-regionality purchasing philosophy.
wine_bars.description: Rudolf Gottwald's Vinothek at the western gateway of the Wachau in Melk, founded 1999. The shop selects exclusively from the immediate region and offers sit-in tastings; an ideal first stop for visitors arriving from Vienna by train.

wine_bars.name: Heuriger Mang
wine_bars.address: Landstraße 38, 3610 Weißenkirchen in der Wachau, Austria
wine_bars.wine_program: Own-estate Weißenkirchen Grüner Veltliner and Riesling with daily cold platter service. Traditional Heuriger format open from midday on the Landstraße.
wine_bars.description: A traditional family Heuriger on the main road through Weißenkirchen, open from noon for wine and cold platters. The sun garden backed by vineyards makes this a popular stop on the Wachau cycling route.
